candidates.why: Exact balance-sheet cash line; preferred over cash flow ending cash.

candidates.row_label: Cash and cash equivalents at the beginning of the period
candidates.why: Beginning cash, not the period-end balance sheet cash target.

candidates.row_label: Net decrease/increase in cash and cash equivalents
candidates.why: Flow, not stock.

candidates.why: Adjustment line, not cash balance.
Not cash and equivalents.

candidates.why: Exact match for total current liabilities.
Balancing line, not the total.

Balancing line, not LCT.

candidates.why: Current borrowings line in the current liabilities section; best clean total for short-term debt excluding leases.
Current portion of long-term debt; useful only if no clean current borrowings total exists, but here likely overlaps with current borrowings.
Possible component of current borrowings, but would risk double counting if combined with total current borrowings.
Possible component of current borrowings, but likely included in current borrowings total.
Possible debt component but appears separate/zero and not needed when total current borrowings exists.

variables.notes: Chose the 'Borrowings' line located within current liabilities as the clean current debt total. Excluded lease liabilities per rule and avoided adding maturities/components to prevent double counting.

candidates.why: Direct match for current taxes payable/current tax liabilities.
Cash flow item, not taxes payable on balance sheet.
Tax asset, opposite of payable.
